title: Find the anomaly
description:
Amaka rolls her chair over: "Three logs came in overnight — auth.log, vpn.log, proxy.log — all from gateways we front Sankofa with. Ninety-nine percent of the traffic is Nigerian: 41.x, 102.x, 105.x prefixes. Somewhere in those three files there is exactly one successful login from a non-Nigerian source IP. Find it."

Click through the three tabs. Narrow the noise with a grep-style filter (try something like `accepted` or `success`). Once you spot the foreign source IP on a successful-auth line, read the trailing session metadata attached to that line — that is the flag. Submit it wrapped in TRAN{} exactly as it appears.

Timestamps are consistent across logs so you can correlate the event if you want to triple-check.

widgetConfig.instructions: Three gateway logs, one correlated event. Most source IPs sit inside Nigerian ASN ranges (41.x, 102.x, 105.x). Exactly one successful authentication originates from outside that footprint — its session field carries the TRAN{} token you submit.
